title: Level 6.99

{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 6.99: "Umbral Light"}}__NOTOC__ __NOEDITSECTION__ {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Work+Sans:ital,wght@0,400;0,500;0,600;1,400;1,500&display=swap'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: GFS Didot}}{{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-link-color: #808080; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-link-color: #808080; } }} {{CSS|content= } .gray { -webkit-filter: grayscale(100%); filter: grayscale(100%); } html { -webkit-filter: grayscale(100%); filter: grayscale(100%); } }} {{CSS|User:Egglord1/TheVoid.css}} {{Blurbackground|file=umbrallight.png}}
[[File:Untitled205 20240925204431.png|200px|center]]
'''“'''

As I set foot in this new realm beyond [[Level 6|the dark halls]], I was met with the sight of nothing but the muted cosmos over a tenebrous world, the feeble lantern in my hand failing to illuminate my environment just as it had in the area before.

The darkness was suffocating.

LEVEL '''{{{no|6.99}}}'''
THREAT INDEX
'''Class Undetermined'''
'''Exit:''' 4/5
Very Hard To Exit
'''Environment:''' 3/5
Perilous Environment
'''Entities:''' ?/5
Undocumented Entities
{{Sub-Class|border=red|background=#454545|border=#ffffff|subclass=Abyssal|description=Extremely Low Visibility}}
Overview
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} {{Blurimage|file=umbrallight.png|caption=''Lightless Heavens'', a photograph of Level 6.99's sky taken by Parker Firdaus, a photographer within the Backrooms who's striking images have become quite popular following the recent absence of [[Entity 1|The Cameraman]].|width=290px}} The Umbral Light. Being a sublevel of [[Level 6]] and one of the most infamous levels of the Main Nine (in no small part due to its pervasive anomalous properties), '''Level 6.99''' is one of the most mysterious levels to have been documented, with much about it still being unknown to this very day. Along with the remaining levels further past it, this place serves as a significant barrier to navigation and exploration past the Main Nine as a whole. Entering the level, one will immediately be met with a seemingly endless expanse of what can be assumed to be the dead remnants of a conventional natural area, filled with forests of towering, dead, evergreens, fields of blackened grass left to decay, and massive expanses of shallow, stagnant water, all under the view of a surreal sky embellished with [[Phenomenon 8|various]] [[H.K.|stars]] and objects which cannot be properly identified. Dotted sparsely across this locale are randomly placed derelict structures of various shapes and sizes, including but not limited to stone paths with lit lamp posts, vacant skyscrapers, eerie suburban homes, empty highways, rotting log cabins, and many more, all seemingly having been abandoned for extended periods of time. Strangely, Level 6.99 is affected by a mysterious visual anomaly; although light can be emitted and is visible, all matter within the level, whether native to it or not, will be unable to reflect said light. As a result of this, despite the skylight within the area, the entirety of the level proper is shrouded in complete darkness, with any light source brought into the level failing to illuminate one's surroundings. In addition to the perils posed by this abnormality, temperatures within the locale average at around -10°F/23.3°C, meaning that succumbing to hypothermia is an ever present risk within the Umbral Light. Additionally, the significant lack of illumination, along with a lack of ambience within the level, tends to caused an increase in audiovisual hallucinations, so much so that it has become persistent throughout all accounts. Most hallucinations consist of loud clicking or banging, coupled with the distant sound of wind chimes. Most hallucinations within the level tend to only be audible in the far distance, out of reach to wanderers. Visual illusions, on the other hand, consist of solely distant lights, which often blink and contort in a haphazard manner.
Sentient Presence & Anomalies
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} There certainly ''is'' something within Level 6.99. It is known for a fact that the vast majority of wanderers who enter the Umbral Light never return, their bodies lost to the darkness. Documentation collected by the [[Backroom Colonists]] have reported moving lights seen far off in the distance and strange noises heard, with photographic and audio evidence to prove that these are not merely instances of the level’s iconic hallucinations. Longterm inhabitants have confirmed the existence of lifeforms within the space, but the excessive darkness within the Umbral Light and the probable hostility of said beings makes properly identifying them a fool’s errand.
Human Habitation
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} Despite the harsh conditions of this space, humanity finds a way even through perils such as this. Due to the lack of safe exits from the Umbral Light, various small communities have settled within its wilderness, often using one of the various pre-existing structures within the level or hiding within the Depths for shelter. Collecting wood from the dead trees within Level 6.99, these communities create fires within the level for both warmth and as a visual marker for their colony’s location, feeding on naturally occurring [[Object 10|Backshrooms]] within the level and boiling the water from the shallow pseudo-oceans within the level for hydration. Due to the relative isolation of most settlements within the space, human society and culture within the level is radically different from [[The Frontrooms]] or culture in the Backrooms. According to testimonies of those born and raised within Level 6.99, many religious groups within the level exist, convinced that the sky of the Umbral Light holds spiritual significance.
'''“'''

Life within that place is ...different, to say the least. When you're growing up as a kid in there, you're almost always in this little isolated bubble of civilization in the sea of darkness, barely aware of anything outside of the Umbral Light. Hell, I didn't even know people had faces until I turned twelve.

But I digress. Anyways, me and the other kids would mostly spend our time playing around the medium-sized settlement the adults had built, could have sworn the outpost had a couple hundred living in it. They didn't let us get too far from home, though.

People tended to disappear around these parts, and we were better off paranoid than dead.

Entrances & Exits
{{Underline|FFFFFF}} ==='''Entrances'''=== * '''Main Entrance:''' Traveling upwards through a staircase in [[Level 6]] will lead here. * Alternatively, wandering far enough into the darkness of outdoor levels may lead one here, more commonplace in levels that are either extraordinarily dark or [[Phenomenon 12|influenced by outside forces]].
{{Blurquote |image=umbrallight.png|size=2500px |quote-text=
Darkened Polaris: An Analysis of the Umbral Light
In all my travels throughout the Backrooms in search of knowledge, Level 6.99 has always uniquely intrigued me. The juxtaposition of such a space naturally invites a sort of wonder; a world filled with light yet enveloped entirely in darkness is quite an interesting case, even in a realm like the Backrooms. Perhaps what draws me to it is a sense of being able to reach the forbidden, a sort of urge to chart the lands where few men have returned. One might call it foolishness, another devotion, but regardless, I eventually caved in to that desire. Taking a team of six with me and equipping ourselves with night vision google to see in the darkness, I entered the Umbral Light on a research expedition, intending to uncover more information about this enigmatic space. Initially, the exploration went well. We had yet to encounter any hostile entities along our journey, and our men were entirely unharmed. However, this was soon to change. While wandering through the dead woodlands of the level, we encountered an entity unlike any we had seen before. An emaciated humanoid being, over ten meters in height, noticed us, and quickly began its pursuit with immense speed. All the other people along with me perished by the hand of this ...''thing''. The only reason I survived was because while it was distracted ripping apart another one of the explorers with me, I had ran, and by pure accident, fell into a cave opening on the surface. Going deeper into the cavity in hopes of evading the monster which had almost slaughtered us all, I quickly found that I was lost within the cave system. Taking off the night vision google and turning on my flashlight in the hope that I had unknowingly transitioned into [[Level 8]], I was disappointed to see that the flashlight still failed to illuminate my surroundings. Putting the night vision goggles back on and wandering deeper into the caverns, I found what appeared to be an aged tablet, written on with some sort of glowing material to counteract the darkness of the space. Taking it with me, soon I managed to escape the area, entering [[Level 8]] and making it to a more safe level from there. However, what I found on the tablet has only served to stoke my curiosity even more. As it was written in a language I cannot yet decipher, I am unaware of the contents of its text, but I still have a lingering feeling that it contains something I'm missing. For further study, I have transcribed the text here.
Από τότε που η μάστιγα έσβησε τον ήλιο μας, το σκοτάδι είναι συντριπτικό. Οι σύντροφοί μου ήδη λιμοκτονούν, καθώς οι σοδειές μας έχουν αποτύχει και οι αδύναμες προσπάθειές μας να ξαναφωτίσουμε την πόλη μας έχουν ως αποτέλεσμα το σκοτάδι να καταβροχθίζει ό,τι φως παρέχουν. Δεν νομίζω ότι θα επιμείνουμε άλλο στην πράξη. Σήμερα, εγώ και μερικοί άλλοι πολίτες σχεδιάζουμε να εκκενώσουμε τα άπειρα σπήλαια. Αν δεν επιβιώσουμε, τα τελευταία μου λόγια: Ζήτω η αυτοκρατορία της Απέζου, και είθε όποιος την βλάψει να είναι καταραμένος για όλη την αιωνιότητα.
}}
